strategy: Buy $200/$215 Call Spread — Aug 2026 (clean entry, earnings July 23)

wheelStrategy.ineligibleReason: Stock price $200.70 — 1 CSP contract requires ~$20,070 cash. Exceeds $10,000 per-contract threshold for a standard retail portfolio. Use Calls/Spreads strategy only.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering premium-selling positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: N/A — Wheel not applicable for this stock

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter now or on any dip to $192–194 (lower support). IV at 48% is moderate — spread structure ($200/$215) keeps net debit manageable. Q1 2026 beat already in. Clean window until July earnings.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 60–80% of max spread gain. First target: stock at $210–212 for ~$10 spread intrinsic. Second target: $215+ for full $15 spread value (~200% max gain).
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not cleared $205+ by ~June 15 (50% of time elapsed on Sep position), close. Spread decays toward zero as both legs approach worthlessness.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 50% loss on net debit paid. Spread's limited risk reduces urgency but do not hold past 50% loss.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by July 18 (5 days before July 23 Q2 earnings) — IV crush will compress spread value rapidly.

options.bestFor: Marginal — 28 days at July 18 exit is below the 30-day green threshold. Workable but theta accelerates sharply in the final month.
Recommended — 63 days of time value at the July 18 exit. Gives DLR room to grind toward $210 on Asia-Pacific expansion news. Low IV (48%) keeps spread cost fair.
Too much time for a stock with modest 7% upside to analyst target. Spread is expensive relative to the potential reward.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — Clean Entry Window, Low IV, Earnings 81 Days Away
recommendation.entryNote: Clean entry window: earnings are July 23 (81 days away), so Aug expiry gives full pre-earnings exposure without IV crush risk. Low IV environment (~22–26%) for a REIT makes premiums fair — no spread required by the IV rule, but spread used to fit $700 budget. Q1 2026 beat: $2.04 EPS vs. $1.94 estimated (+5.1%); revenue grew 16% YoY. Stock near 52-week high ($208.14). Limited upside to average analyst target (~$208) means this is more of a moderate trade — 1–2 spreads max. Asia expansion (Japan, Singapore) is the differentiated growth driver vs. peers.

exitPlan.label: Spread 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $210–212 (+5%) on continued data centre demand strength

exitPlan.label: Spread 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $215+ (+7%) on new ATH push or Asia contract announcements

companyOverview: Digital Realty Trust is a global data centre REIT specializing in wholesale/hyperscale colocation — large-footprint facilities for hyperscalers and enterprises requiring dedicated power and space. It operates across North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific. The AI buildout is its primary tailwind: hyperscalers need more data centre capacity and are signing long-term leases. Q1 2026 results were a beat: EPS $2.04 vs. $1.94 expected, revenue and adjusted EBITDA grew 16%. The 2026 guidance calls for 8% Core FFO growth with a $10B+ development pipeline. Key differentiator: aggressive expansion in Asia Pacific — new campus in Japan, significant growth in Singapore — where data centre demand is outstripping local supply. Stock is trading near its 52-week high of $208.14, suggesting the market is already pricing in near-term fundamentals. Vanguard holds 7.33% stake.

Beat $1.94 estimate by 5.1%; revenue +16% YoY — strong quarter

Avg target: ~$208; current price $200.70 — only 3.6% to consensus
Low IV REIT — premium fair; spread used for budget management
High institutional ownership = stable, less speculative float

insiderFlow.summary: Minimal activity — director grants (non-cash) and one older sell in December 2025 at lower price. No recent open-market buying. Neutral signal.

Sold at $157 when stock was much lower. Has since appreciated 27% — not a negative recent signal.

insiderFlow.signal: NEUTRAL — no open-market buying. Director sold at $157 in December (stock now $201). Grant activity only in 2026. Insiders own ~0.17% — low conviction stake.

catalysts: Q1 2026 beat (+5.1% EPS) shows fundamental momentum — consecutive beats set up a re-rating toward $220+
Asia Pacific expansion: new Japan data centre + Singapore pipeline taps into AI infrastructure demand in undersupplied markets
$10B+ development pipeline provides multi-year revenue visibility and AFFO growth runway
8% Core FFO growth guidance for 2026 — sustainable, not cyclical
Hyperscaler demand: MSFT, GOOG, AMZN all expanding data centre footprints; DLR is a primary wholesale landlord
Vanguard 7.33% institutional stake provides stable ownership base; limited speculative float
risks: Limited upside to consensus: avg analyst target is $208, only 3.6% from current price. Modest potential return unless targets revise upward
Stock near 52-week high ($208) — not a 'discount' play; little margin of safety in current setup
No recent insider buying: 0.17% insider ownership signals executives don't view this as a high-conviction personal investment
Rising interest rates: REITs are rate-sensitive — any Fed hawkishness reprices DLR on valuation
July 23 earnings: Q2 guidance miss or FFO guidance reduction could reverse the Q1 momentum
Customer concentration: top hyperscaler tenants represent large % of revenue; any large tenant decision to in-source could impact occupancy
dilution: Typical REIT equity structure. Periodic equity issuance for development pipeline funding. $10B+ pipeline is capital-intensive — watch for new equity offerings. No acute near-term dilution concern.
